Name: gradient_boosting_baseline
Title: Gradient Boosting Baseline with Feature Engineering
Strategy: Use LightGBM/XGBoost gradient boosting with basic feature engineering as a strong baseline. Gradient boosting consistently performs well on tabular data competitions.
Preprocessing: Handle missing values with median/mode imputation
Encode categorical variables with label encoding
Feature_Engineering: Interaction features between top correlated features
Aggregation statistics (mean, std, min, max) for grouped features
Expected_Strengths: Strong baseline for tabular data, handles mixed types well, fast training
Expected_Weaknesses: May not capture complex non-linear relationships, limited for image/text data

Name: neural_network_approach
Title: Neural Network with Embedding Layers
Strategy: Use a neural network with learned embeddings for categorical features. This can capture complex non-linear relationships that tree-based models miss.
Feature_Engineering: Numerical feature interactions via hidden layers
Implementation_Plan: Implement training loop with early stopping
Expected_Strengths: Can capture complex patterns, flexible architecture
Expected_Weaknesses: Requires more data to train well, slower than tree-based models

Name: ensemble_approach
Title: Weighted Ensemble of Diverse Models
Strategy: Combine predictions from multiple diverse models (LightGBM, XGBoost, CatBoost, Neural Net) using weighted averaging or stacking to improve robustness and score.
Preprocessing: Handle missing values appropriately for each model
Implementation_Plan: Generate out-of-fold predictions for each model
Optimize ensemble weights using validation score
Expected_Strengths: Reduces variance, combines diverse model strengths, robust
Expected_Weaknesses: More complex, longer training time, harder to debug

Name: automl_baseline
Title: AutoML Baseline with Auto-sklearn or FLAML
Strategy: Use an AutoML framework to automatically search for the best model and hyperparameters. Good for quick baselines and when the problem type is standard.
Expected_Strengths: Quick to implement, explores many model types automatically
Expected_Weaknesses: Less control over model, may not find domain-specific features
